# UC-17.1.71 · Cisco ISE Admin Account Lockout, Failed-Login Burst, and Privileged-Role Audit

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE admins control who can connect to the network. We watch when admin accounts get locked out, when someone fails login many times, and any change to admin privileges — these are big-deal events.*

---

## Description

Detects: (a) Admin login bursts from a single source IP (>5 failures in 5 min, suggesting brute force). (b) Admin account lockouts. (c) Any admin-role change (add/modify/remove). (d) Any super-admin role grant.

## Value

ISE admin role changes alter who can change network access policy — a high-privilege control. Detecting role changes provides audit evidence for SOX/PCI; brute-force attempts indicate active attacker reconnaissance.

## Implementation

Schedule every 5 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger 6 failed admin logins from one IP. UC-17.1.71 fires.

### Negative scenario

Normal admin login. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60006, 60010, 60050, 60051, 60052, 60100)
| stats count, values(admin_user) as users, values(admin_role) as roles, values(source_ip) as ips by MessageCode
| where (MessageCode=="60006" AND count > 5) OR MessageCode IN (60010, 60050, 60051, 60052, 60100)
```

## Visualization

Admin login failure trend; role-change timeline; super-admin grants log.

## Known False Positives

- Bulk role changes during planned admin reorg.
- Admin accidentally locked out via repeated typo.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Administrative Operations](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
